name: Amazon VPC
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for VPC networking, traffic, and exposure controls.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track network traffic, rejected flows, NAT gateway health, endpoint usage, and subnet capacity.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Use flow logs, route tables, and reachability analysis to explain network paths and denied traffic.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure network availability, IP capacity, route correctness, and endpoint reliability.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Validate segmentation, least-exposed security groups, private subnet patterns, and threat findings.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate public exposure, route drift, security group changes, and traffic anomalies.

sections.name: Network Overview
sections.purpose: VPCs, subnets, route tables, gateways, endpoints, and IP utilization.

sections.name: Flow Log Traffic
sections.purpose: Accepted and rejected flows, top talkers, ports, protocols, and cross-zone patterns.

sections.name: Internet and NAT Egress
sections.purpose: NAT gateway bytes, packets, errors, port allocation, and public egress volume.

sections.name: Private Connectivity
sections.purpose: VPC endpoint usage, private route coverage, and endpoint policy posture.

sections.name: Threat and Exposure
sections.purpose: GuardDuty network findings, open security groups, public routes, and Config rule status.

sections.name: Change Evidence
sections.purpose: CloudTrail events for route tables, security groups, NACLs, gateways, and endpoints.

metrics.name: BytesOutToDestination
metrics.why: Track NAT egress volume and cost pressure.

metrics.name: PacketsDropCount
metrics.why: Track dropped traffic when Network Firewall is used.

metrics.name: AvailableIpAddressCount
metrics.why: Detect subnet IP exhaustion before deployments fail.

log_queries.name: Rejected VPC flows
log_queries.intent: Find blocked network traffic in VPC Flow Logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, srcAddr, dstAddr, dstPort, protocol, action | filter action = "REJECT" | stats count() by srcAddr, dstAddr, dstPort | sort count desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: Top egress destinations
log_queries.query: fields srcAddr, dstAddr, bytes, action | filter action = "ACCEPT" | stats sum(bytes) as totalBytes by dstAddr | sort totalBytes desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: Security group changes
log_queries.intent: Find security group ingress and egress rule changes in CloudTrail logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.arn, requestParameters.groupId | filter eventSource = "ec2.amazonaws.com" and eventName like /AuthorizeSecurityGroup|RevokeSecurityGroup|ModifySecurityGroup/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: NATGatewayPortAllocationErrors
alarms.action: Review NAT gateway scaling, destination concentration, and endpoint alternatives.

alarms.name: SubnetIpCapacityLow
alarms.action: Add subnet capacity or rebalance workload placement.

alarms.name: FlowLogRejectSpike
alarms.condition: Rejected flow count spikes above baseline
alarms.action: Review route tables, NACLs, security groups, and recent network changes.

alarms.name: PublicExposureChange
alarms.condition: CloudTrail detects public route or open security group change
alarms.action: Trigger security review and validate approved change ticket.

security_controls.name: Flow logs enabled
security_controls.evidence: VPC Flow Logs are enabled for production VPCs and retained centrally.

security_controls.name: Least-exposed security groups
security_controls.evidence: Security groups avoid broad inbound access except approved public entry points.

security_controls.name: Private subnet pattern
security_controls.evidence: Application workloads use private subnets and expose traffic through approved gateways or load balancers.

security_controls.name: Config network rules
security_controls.evidence: AWS Config checks public routes, open security groups, flow logs, and encryption-related controls.

security_controls.name: GuardDuty network findings
security_controls.evidence: GuardDuty is enabled and findings are routed to Security Hub or incident workflow.

adoption_plan: Enable VPC Flow Logs for production VPCs.
Create dashboards for rejected flows, NAT health, subnet capacity, and security findings.
Create alarms for NAT errors, subnet IP capacity, reject spikes, and public exposure changes.
Review security groups, route tables, NACLs, and VPC endpoint policies.
Run reachability analysis for critical private service paths.
Promote the pack as the standard network observability baseline.
